Class Sequent

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.sequent.Sequent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<SequentFormula>, SyntaxElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
Sequent.NILSequent
public abstract class Sequent extends Object implements Iterable<SequentFormula>, SyntaxElement

The Sequent class represents a formal proof construct consisting of two components: an antecedent and a succedent, which are both instances of Semisequent. It provides an abstraction for handling proof sequents in a logic-based reasoning framework.

A Sequent has the general structure antecedent ==> succedent, where: The antecedent and the succedent represents collections of formulas.

Instances of this class are immutable, ensuring that operations such as adding, replacing, or removing formulas result in the creation of new Sequent objects rather than modifying existing ones.

Subclassing

Subclasses of Sequent must implement abstract methods to define specific construction and behavior, including:

  • getEmptySequent() - Returns a canonical empty sequent.
  • createSequent(Semisequent,Semisequent) - Constructs a new sequent from given antecedent and succedent components.
  • createSemisequent(ImmutableList) - Constructs a Semisequent from a list of formulas.

Immutability

All mutating operations on a sequent (e.g., adding, removing, or replacing formulas) return a new Sequent instance without altering the original object.

See Also:
  • Semisequent
  • SequentFormula
  • ImmutableList
  • SequentChangeInfo
  • PosInOccurrence

    Constructor
    Description
    protected
    Sequent(Semisequent emptySeq)
    Constructs a new empty Sequent object, used for Sequent.NILSequent implementations.
    protected
    Sequent(Semisequent antecedent, Semisequent succedent)
    Constructs a new Sequent object with the specified antecedent and succedent.

    Method

    Adds a formula to the antecedent or succedent of this sequent depending on the inAntecedent flag.

    Adds a formula to the sequent at the given position.

    Adds a list formulas to the antecedent or succedent of this sequent depending on the value of the inAntecedent flag.

    adds the formulas of list insertions to the sequent starting at position pos.

    Retrieves the semisequent representing the antecedent of this sequent.

    Returns a list containing every SequentFormula in this sequent.

    changeFormula(SequentFormula newCF, PosInOccurrence p)
    replaces the formula at the given position with another one (NOTICE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
    SequentChangeInfo
    changeFormula(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements, PosInOccurrence pos)
    replaces the formula at position pos with the head of the given list and adds the remaining list elements to the sequent (NOTE: Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)

  • Constructor Details

    • Sequent

      protected Sequent(Semisequent antecedent, Semisequent succedent)
      Constructs a new Sequent object with the specified antecedent and succedent. The sequent represents a logical relationship of the form antecedent ==> succedent.
      Parameters:
      antecedent - the Semisequent representing the antecedent of the sequent
      succedent - the Semisequent representing the succedent of the sequent
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if both the antecedent and succedent are empty

    • Sequent

      protected Sequent(Semisequent emptySeq)
      Constructs a new empty Sequent object, used for Sequent.NILSequent implementations.
      Parameters:
      emptySeq - an empty Semisequent, used as both antecedent and succedent
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if the provided semisequent is not empty

  • Method Details

    • size

      public int size()
      Computes the total number of formulas in the sequent by summing the sizes of the antecedent and succedent.
      Returns:
      the total number of formulas in the sequent

    • antecedent

      public Semisequent antecedent()
      Retrieves the semisequent representing the antecedent of this sequent.
      Returns:
      the Semisequent representing the antecedent

    • succedent

      public Semisequent succedent()
      Retrieves the semisequent representing the succedent of this sequent.
      Returns:
      the Semisequent representing the succedent

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Provides a string representation of this sequent in the form: antecedent ==> succedent.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of the sequent

    • iterator

      public @NonNull Iterator<SequentFormula> iterator()
      Creates an iterator to traverse all SequentFormula objects in this sequent. The iterator traverses the antecedent first, followed by the succedent.
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<SequentFormula>
      Returns:
      an iterator over the formulas in this sequent

    • numberInAntecedent

      public boolean numberInAntecedent(int formulaNumber)
      Determines whether the formula at the specified position is part of the antecedent.
      Parameters:
      formulaNumber - the 1-based index of the formula (1-based means the first formula has index 1)
      Returns:
      true if the formula is in the antecedent, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the formula number is out of bounds

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks whether this sequent contains any formulas.
      Returns:
      true if the sequent is empty, false otherwise

    • getFormulaByNr

      public SequentFormula getFormulaByNr(int formulaNumber)
      Retrieves the formula at the specified 1-based index in the sequent. Formulas in the antecedent are indexed first, followed by those in the succedent. 1-based means the index of the first formula is 1.
      Parameters:
      formulaNumber - the 1-based index of the formula to retrieve
      Returns:
      the SequentFormula at the specified position
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the formula number is out of bounds

    • addFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo addFormula(SequentFormula sequentFormula, boolean inAntecedent, boolean first)
      Adds a formula to the antecedent or succedent of this sequent depending on the inAntecedent flag. The formula can be added either at the beginning or the end, based on the first flag. (NOTE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - the SequentFormula to add
      inAntecedent - true to add the formula to the antecedent, false for the succedent
      first - true to add the formula at the beginning, false to add it at the end of the selected semisequent
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo containing the modified sequent and details of the change like which formulas have been added or removed (removal is possible as the implementation guarantee redundancy freeness)

    • addFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo addFormula(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions, boolean inAntecedent, boolean first)

      Adds a list formulas to the antecedent or succedent of this sequent depending on the value of the inAntecedent flag. The formulas can be added either at the beginning or the end of the semisequent, based on the first flag.

      (NOTE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)

      Parameters:
      insertions - an ImmutableList of SequentFormulas to add
      inAntecedent - true to add the formulas to the antecedent, false for the succedent
      first - true to add the formulas at the beginning, false to add them at the end
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo containing the modified sequent and which formulas have been added or removed

    • composeSequent

      protected Sequent composeSequent(boolean inAntecedent, Semisequent otherSemisequent)
      Replaces the antecedent or succedent of this sequent with a specified Semisequent.
      Parameters:
      inAntecedent - true to replace the antecedent, false for the succedent
      otherSemisequent - the Semisequent to use as the replacement
      Returns:
      a new Sequent with the updated antecedent or succedent

    • getEmptySequent

      protected abstract Sequent getEmptySequent()
      Returns an instance representing the empty sequent.
      Returns:
      an empty sequent

    • createSequent

      protected abstract Sequent createSequent(Semisequent newAntecedent, Semisequent newSuccedent)
      Creates a new sequent of the shape newAntecedent ==> newSuccedent. Subclassing methods should check whether both semisequents are empty and in that case return the sequent provided by getEmptySequent().
      Parameters:
      newAntecedent - the Semisequent to be used as antecedent
      newSuccedent - the Semisequent to be used succedent
      Returns:
      a Sequent composed of the provided semisequents

    • addFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo addFormula(SequentFormula sequentFormula, PosInOccurrence pos)

      Adds a formula to the sequent at the given position.

      (NOTE: Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)

      Parameters:
      sequentFormula - a SequentFormula to be added
      pos - a PosInOccurrence describes position in the sequent
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • createSemisequent

      protected abstract Semisequent createSemisequent(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> formulas)
      Creates a new Semisequent composed of the list of formulas proved as argument
      Parameters:
      formulas - the ImmutableList of SequentFormulas used to create the semisequent
      Returns:
      the Semisequent consisting of the provided formulas

    • replaceFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo replaceFormula(int formulaNr, SequentFormula replacement)
      Replaces a formula at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      formulaNr - where to replace the formula
      replacement - the new sequent formula
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • addFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo addFormula(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> insertions, PosInOccurrence pos)
      adds the formulas of list insertions to the sequent starting at position pos. (NOTE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
      Parameters:
      insertions - an ImmutableList of SequentFormulas with the formulas to be added
      pos - the PosInOccurrence describing the position where to insert the formulas
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • removeFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo removeFormula(PosInOccurrence pos)
      removes the formula at position pos (NOTE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
      Parameters:
      pos - a PosInOccurrence that describes position in the sequent
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • changeFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo changeFormula(ImmutableList<SequentFormula> replacements, PosInOccurrence pos)
      replaces the formula at position pos with the head of the given list and adds the remaining list elements to the sequent (NOTE: Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
      Parameters:
      replacements - the ImmutableList of SequentFormulas whose head replaces the formula at position pos and adds the rest of the list behind the changed formula
      pos - a PosInOccurrence describing the position of the formula to be replaced
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • changeFormula

      public SequentChangeInfo changeFormula(SequentFormula newCF, PosInOccurrence p)
      replaces the formula at the given position with another one (NOTICE:Sequent determines index using identity (==) not equality.)
      Parameters:
      newCF - the SequentFormula replacing the old one
      p - a PosInOccurrence describes position in the sequent
      Returns:
      a SequentChangeInfo which contains the new sequent and information which formulas have been added or removed

    • formulaNumberInSequent

      public int formulaNumberInSequent(boolean inAntecedent, SequentFormula sequentFormula)
      Computes the position of the given sequent formula on the proof sequent, starting with one for the very first sequent formula.
      Parameters:
      inAntecedent - a boolean stating whether we search in the antecedent or the succedent
      sequentFormula - the given sequent formula
      Returns:
      an integer strictly greater than zero for the position of the given sequent formula on the proof sequent.

    • formulaNumberInSequent

      public int formulaNumberInSequent(PosInOccurrence pio)
      Computes the position of the given PosInOccurrence on the proof sequent.
      Parameters:
      pio - the position
      Returns:
      an integer strictly greater than zero for the position of the given sequent formula on the proof sequent.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(@Nullable Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • contains

      public boolean contains(SequentFormula formula)
      used to check whether this sequent contains a given sequent formula.
      Parameters:
      formula - the given formula
      Returns:
      true if this sequent contains the given formula

    • asList

      public ImmutableList<SequentFormula> asList()
      Returns a list containing every SequentFormula in this sequent.
      Returns:
      a list containing every SequentFormula in this sequent.

    • getChildCount

      public int getChildCount()
      Specified by:
      getChildCount in interface SyntaxElement
      Returns:
      the number of children of this syntax element.

      public @NonNull SyntaxElement getChild(int n)
      Description copied from interface: SyntaxElement
      Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
      Specified by:
      getChild in interface SyntaxElement
      Parameters:

      Returns:
